overview: Selesnya Landfall is an aggressive Standard variant that leverages a white splash for sideboard tools and early-game acceleration. It utilizes Fetch Lands and Icetill Explorer to maximize Landfall triggers, growing threats like Mossborn Hydra and Tifa Lockhart while generating card advantage through Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam and Felidar Retreat.
win_conditions: Mossborn Hydra growing large with Trample
Felidar Retreat token swarm and board pressure
Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam resource generation and board presence

keep_criteria: Hands with early land drops and Fetch Lands
Hands containing early threats like Sazh's Chocobo or Bristly Bill, Spine Sower
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking land drops or early acceleration
Hands heavy on late-game cards without early plays

examples.reason: Provides early board presence and Landfall triggers to establish pressure quickly.

early_game: Play Fetch Lands to trigger Landfall and find basics
Drop early threats like Sazh's Chocobo and Bristly Bill, Spine Sower
Use Icetill Explorer to guarantee Landfall and reuse Fetch Lands
mid_game: Double Landfall triggers with Traveling Chocobo for explosive plays
Grow Mossborn Hydra and Tifa Lockhart with +1/+1 counters
Cast Felidar Retreat to generate tokens and pump the board
late_game: Rely on Bristly Bill, Spine Sower's late-game ability to dominate
Use Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam to generate card advantage and extra bodies
Apply sideboard answers like Dawn's Truce or Requisition Raid to close out matches

key_cards.name: Tifa Lockhart
key_cards.notes: Originally used for combo-kill potential, now serves as a secondary win condition supported by pumps and Veils.

key_cards.name: Sazh's Chocobo
key_cards.notes: Becomes a large creature quickly with Fetch Lands and Bristly Bill, shifting focus to permanent power.

key_cards.name: Mossborn Hydra
key_cards.notes: Starts small but grows rapidly with Landfall and Bristly Bill, eventually gaining Trample to win on its own.

key_cards.name: Bristly Bill, Spine Sower
key_cards.notes: The deck's glue that boosts all creatures on land drops and has a powerful late-game ability.

key_cards.name: Traveling Chocobo
key_cards.notes: Doubles Landfall triggers for explosive plays and occasional surprise hit-kills.

key_cards.name: Felidar Retreat
key_cards.notes: Prevents board wipes, generates tokens, and pumps creatures to maintain pressure.

key_cards.name: Icetill Explorer
key_cards.notes: Acts as extra Traveling Chocobo copies, guarantees Landfall, and reuses Fetch Lands.

key_cards.name: Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam
key_cards.notes: Provides resources, trample, and can sacrifice counters to draw cards and create tokens.

key_cards.name: Fetch Lands
key_cards.notes: Enables multiple Landfall triggers per turn and finds basic lands for Icetill Explorer.

sequencing_tips: Play Fetch Lands before other lands to maximize Landfall triggers
Preserve Fetch Lands in hand when needed for key creatures like Mossborn Hydra
Use Icetill Explorer to reuse Fetch Lands and guarantee triggers
Remove +1/+1 counters from creatures to activate Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam for card advantage when damage isn't the priority
Cast Felidar Retreat to protect the board and generate tokens when opponents have removal

matchups.advice: Side in Sheltered by Ghosts to hold the opponent's clock while interacting with the board, removing blockers or larger threats.
Use Surrak, Elusive Hunter to bypass Pinnacle Starcage and punish spot removal with its built-in protection.
Bring in Sheltered by Ghosts to control the board and hold the opponent's clock while interacting with their token swarm.
Side in Frenzied Baloth for immediate impact and Requisition Raid to deal with artifacts/enchantments while buffing your board.
Side in Rest in Peace to shut down graveyards and disrupt their combo or value engines.
Use Sheltered by Ghosts to hold the clock and interact with the board in the mirror match.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Although this is an Aggro deck, we need to use Fetch Lands to enable more Landfall triggers each turn... Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam offers an element I've always missed when playing this archetype in other versions: resources.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Tifa became another win condition rather than the archetype's primary game plan. She still receives support from Bristly Bill, Spine Sower and Snakeskin Veil , but winning with hit-kills has become less of a priority.

A Fetch or a Bristly Bill mix with it can turn any land drop into a 4/4, and maybe even a 10/10 the next turn with Trample. It's the kind of card that demands an answer or will win the game on its own.
Felidar Retreat complements the Landfall package. It ensures our board doesn't go empty in games where opponents clog their removal lists, while also making it harder to interact.

Dyadrine, Synthesis Amalgam offers an element I've always missed when playing this archetype in other versions: resources. Besides having a decent body for its cost and Trample... it can remove two +1/+1 counters from your creatures to put another body on the board and draw more cards

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: each Fetch will find a basic land, and since we can play two lands per turn, that's four +1/+1 counters on all creatures or four 2/2 tokens
there are many situations where preserving Fetch Lands is important for other cards.
Icetill Explorer works almost like copies 5 and 6 of Traveling Chocobo , but with the bonus of always guaranteeing Landfall for your cards and, most importantly, always reusing Fetch Lands.
it can remove two +1/+1 counters from your creatures to put another body on the board and draw more cards, sometimes more important than the damage we can deal that turn.
Felidar Retreat complements the Landfall package. It ensures our board doesn't go empty in games where opponents clog their removal lists

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Dawn's Truce is the best option we have to protect our creatures from sweepers like Day of Judgment or Zero-Point Ballad .
Surrak, Elusive Hunter... bypasses Pinnacle Starcage , serving as another mid-game threat.
Requisition Raid deals with troublesome artifacts and enchantments like Caretaker's Talent and Overlord of the Hauntwoods or Pinnacle Starcage
